title: bursera inversa
intro: Bursera inversa, also known as the resinous tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Burseraceae family. The plant's resinous sap has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bursera inversa include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic properties.
